description: A plain candid event photograph, not a designed graphic. It captures a military color guard presenting the colors at an outdoor ceremony: soldiers in OCP camouflage march carrying the U.S. national flag and several unit/guidon flags (including a red regimental color), led by a soldier carrying a rifle. Behind them, ranks of soldiers stand in formation along rows of white slip-covered chairs in front of a tan stucco venue building with string lights. On the right, a group of civilian family members and guests in summer dresses stand watching. It is a natural-light color photograph with no overlaid slogan text or graphic design; the only marking is a small faint photographer/venue watermark in the lower-left corner. Per the tweet, this is the Oak Grove Racetrack (Kentucky) naturalization ceremony for active-duty Fort Campbell service members. Purpose is event documentation rather than designed propaganda.
notable_text: (none on image; small venue watermark logo only)
